summary: PubSub test 'ChannelMessage' timed out waiting to receive a message on a Redis pub/sub channel within 1.001 seconds.
root_cause: Test at pubsub_test.go:661 timed out after 1.001s while expecting to receive a message on a channel. The channel remained empty (len:0, cap:10), indicating the expected Redis pub/sub message was not delivered within the timeout window.

evidence.reason: Explicit timeout failure reported by test framework
Test assertion was waiting for data to arrive on the channel but timeout occurred
Channel state shows zero elements, confirming no message was received before timeout

evidence.quote: Ran 1224 of 1274 Specs in 179.248 seconds -- 1223 Passed | 1 Failed
evidence.reason: Only 1 failure among 1224 tests suggests timing sensitivity or flakiness rather than deterministic defect

suggested_fix: Review the pub/sub message delivery latency in the test or implementation. Consider increasing the test timeout threshold in pubsub_test.go:661 or investigating if there are race conditions in message delivery. Verify Redis server responsiveness and check if the test is sensitive to CI runner load.

model_info._backfill_note: Pre-Codex-2026-05-14 F2 fix. The Claude shim now persists _model_info from every API call; this row predates that emit so resolved_model and usage are not recoverable. requested_model is set to the shim-level alias (haiku for v1, sonnet for v2) which is what the post-fix shim emits.
